summary: Gruber investigates the timeline of the High Sierra root login bug, which allowed anyone to log in as root with a blank password. While the bug was publicly disclosed on Twitter on November 28, 2017, it had actually been posted as a workaround on Apple's own developer forums on November 13 by a user who stumbled on it accidentally while trying to fix an unrelated admin account problem. The more troubling implication is that the exploit may have been known to others who kept it quiet. Despite floating around semi-publicly for weeks, no widespread harm appears to have resulted before Apple patched it.
key_points: The root login bug existed since High Sierra shipped on September 25 and possibly through summer betas, but went unnoticed because trying a blank root password is so absurd nobody thought to test it
The exploit was posted on Apple's own developer forums on November 13, two weeks before it went viral, by a user who discovered it accidentally out of frustration
The more insidious concern is not that nobody noticed the bug, but that some who discovered it may have kept it to themselves rather than reporting it
At least one company's IT staff independently found the bug a week before the public disclosure and reported it to Apple on November 23, noting it was already mentioned in several places online
The original discoverer found the suggestion to try root with a blank password on some other forum, meaning the exploit was circulating even more widely than the Apple forums post
money_quotes: Like the classic '1-2-3-4-5' scene in Spaceballs, but with the ultimate weak password — none at all.

More insidious though, is the notion that it might not have escaped notice prior to its widespread publicization yesterday — but that the people who had heretofore discovered it kept it to themselves.
It seemed like the issue had been revealed, but Apple had not noticed yet.
As to how I stumbled on this, the answer is simple. Pure frustration.
So the exploit was floating around, under the radar, for weeks at least, but it seems as though no widespread harm came of it.
key_insight: The High Sierra root login bug wasn't truly hidden — it was posted on Apple's own forums two weeks before going viral, raising uncomfortable questions about Apple's responsiveness to security issues reported through its own channels.
